blocks:
Ayahs

وَٱلَّيْلِ إِذَا يَغْشَىٰ (۱)
By the night when it envelops, (1)
وَٱلنَّهَارِ إِذَا تَجَلَّىٰ (۲)
by the day when it brightens, (2)
وَمَا خَلَقَ ٱلذَّكَرَ وَٱلْأُنثَىٰٓ (۳)
by Him who created the male and the female: (3)
إِنَّ سَعْيَكُمْ لَشَتَّىٰ (٤)
your endeavours are indeed diverse. (4)

Key Points

We read in a Hadith: Allah has the right to swear by whatever He wills among His creation, but people must only swear by Allah. (75) "شتّى" (Shattā) is the plural of "Shatīt," meaning scattered or diverse. The oath is not merely for the difference in efforts, because this difference is obvious to everyone, but rather the oath is due to the varying results and consequences of the deeds. The shade of the night is a great blessing in which temperature moderation, physical rest, spiritual tranquility, and sincere supplications take place.

75) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Time is a sacred matter and worthy of an oath. «والیل اذا یغشى» 2- Pay attention both to the external signs (āfāqī), «والّیل... والنهار» (The Night... and the Day), and to the internal signs (anfusī). «و ما خلق الذكر و الانثى» 3- The system of pairing (zaujiyyah) in animals and humans is a sign of Allah's power and wisdom. «ما خلق الذكر و الانثى» 4- The difference in Allah's mercy or wrath is wise and just, not arbitrary, because your efforts, O humans, are diverse. «ان سیعكم لشتّى»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-Layl

This Surah has 21 verses and was revealed in Mecca. The name of the Surah is taken from the first verse, which begins with an oath by the night when it covers. The oath begins by emphasizing the difference in human efforts and motivations and continues by stating the law of recompense for deeds. This law identifies kindness to others as the basis for ease in affairs, and stinginess and arrogance as the factors that make human matters difficult. The continuation of the Surah refers to the general divine tradition of guidance, stating that the ultimate fate of those who do not heed the divine warning is the Fire of Hell, and the fate of the pious is freedom from it. These are, of course, the pious who give Zakat and have no motivation other than the pleasure of Allah, and Allah will also provide the means for their satisfaction.

Ayahs

فَأَمَّا مَنْ أَعْطَىٰ وَٱتَّقَىٰ (٥)
As for him who gives and is Godwary (5)
وَصَدَّقَ بِٱلْحُسْنَىٰ (٦)
and confirms the best promise, (6)
فَسَنُيَسِّرُهُۥ لِلْيُسْرَىٰ (۷)
We will surely ease him toward facility. (7)
وَأَمَّا مَنۢ بَخِلَ وَٱسْتَغْنَىٰ (۸)
But as for him, who is stingy and self-complacent, (8)
وَكَذَّبَ بِٱلْحُسْنَىٰ (۹)
and denies the best promise, (9)
فَسَنُيَسِّرُهُۥ لِلْعُسْرَىٰ (۱٠)
We will surely ease him toward hardship. (10)
وَمَا يُغْنِى عَنْهُ مَالُهُۥٓ إِذَا تَرَدَّىٰٓ (۱۱)
His wealth will not avail him when he perishes. (11)

Key Points

Perhaps the intent of giving accompanied by taqwa (piety/God-consciousness) is that the giving must be with pure intention, without obligation (or reminder of the favor), from lawful wealth, and in the path of Allah, all of which are encompassed in the word taqwa. «اعطى واتقى» If Allah opens the path of good for some, «فسنیسّره للیسرى», and the path of evil for others, «فسنیسّره للیسرى» (Note: the second quote is likely a typo in the source and should be للعسرى), it is due to the difference in the efforts of the people themselves. «انّ سعیكم لشتّى»

There was a tree in the house of a wealthy person. When the fruit of that tree fell to the ground, the neighbor's children would pick it up and eat it, but the owner of the tree would take the fruit out of the children's mouths. The father of the children reported the matter to the Prophet Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله. The حضرت went to the homeowner and offered to buy the tree from him in exchange for a tree in Paradise. The man refused the Prophet, saying: "I will not sell the immediate world for the deferred Hereafter." The Prophet returned. One of his companions asked: "If I acquire that tree, will You make the same deal with me?" The حضرت said: "Yes." That person managed to buy the tree for a price many times over and placed it at the disposal of the Prophet. The حضرت then went to that poor family and gifted the tree to them. It was at this time that the verses of this Surah were revealed.(76) «فامّا من اعطى و اتقى و صدق بالحسنى...»

Whoever takes the first step of generosity based on faith in the Resurrection and taqwa, Allah smooths his spiritual capacity and conditions for deeds, familiarizes his soul with good work, increases his experience, intellect, and competence, grants him inspiration and innovation, increases his social acceptance, and places new paths for good work before him, all of which are instances of «فسنیسّره للعسرى».

The subtle point is that the Quran says: We prepare him for the easiest path «فسنیسّره للیسرى», and it did not say: We make the task easy for him. «سنیسر له الیسرى». And there is a difference between the person being smooth (or prepared) and his task being smooth. Through reliance (on Allah), supplication, and connection to the infinite power, a person can easily attain such tranquility (sakina) and breadth of heart (sa'at sadr) that he accepts any task easily, and performing any good deed becomes easy for him.

In the history of Karbala, we read that as soon as Zaynab al-Kubra saw the fragmented body of Imam Husayn عليه السلام, she said: «ربّنا تقبّل منّا هذا القلیل» (Our Lord, accept this small offering from us!) O Lord, this martyr is small compared to Your Greatness, accept him! Yes, faith and taqwa insure a person against any incident and make the task smooth and easy. «فسنیسّره للیسرى»

The intent of the words «یُسرى» (Yusra) and «عُسرى» (Usra), which are presented in the feminine form, is easy and difficult deeds or paths.

What is important is the principle of giving; its type and amount are secondary. Giving is sometimes small, sometimes large, sometimes from wealth, and sometimes from status or reputation.

The word «حُسنى» (Husna) is either a verbal noun (masdar) like ruj'a (return) or the feminine form of ahsan (best).

Imam Baqir عليه السلام, in interpreting the verse «و امّا مَن اعطى و اتّقى و صدّق بالحسنى فسنیسّره للیسرى», said: «لا یرید شیئا من الخیر الاّ یسّره اللّه له» (He does not desire anything good except that Allah makes its performance easy for him).(77)

And in interpreting the verse «و امّا من بخل واستغنى و كذّب بالحسنى فسنیسّره للعسرى», He said: «لا یرید شیئا من الشرّ الاّ یسّره اللّه له» (He does not decide upon performing any evil except that Allah makes the performance of that evil easy for him, and he commits it).(78)

76) Tafsir-e Namuneh. 77) Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 46. 78) Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 47.

Messages

1- The correct method of propagation is to state examples and instances after presenting general principles. «انّ سعیكم لشتّى فامّا مَن اعطى»

2- Giving accompanied by taqwa is effective. «اعطى واتقى»

3- Giving must be without hypocrisy (riya), arrogance, obligation (or reminder of the favor), or harm. «اعطى و اتقى ...»

4- What you give is not important; how, with what motivation, and in what state you give is important. «اعطى و اتقى»

5- The condition for the acceptance of giving is taqwa. «اعطى و اتقى» (Otherwise, giving unlawful wealth or giving with impure intention has no reward, because «انّما یتقبّل اللّه من المتّقین»(79))

6- The Hereafter is better than the world. «الحُسنى»

7- Only one who believes in the Resurrection (Ma'ad) can give his offering a divine color and be among the pious. «و صدق بالحُسنى» (The Quran refers to the promises of the Day of Judgment as Husna. «كُلاً وعد اللّه الحُسنى»(80))

8- Faith in the Day of Judgment is a condition for the acceptance of deeds. «و صدق بالحسنى»

9- Stinginess and being arrogant due to wealth are signs of lack of taqwa. «من بخل واستغنى»

10- The opening or complication of a person's affairs is the result of his generosity or stinginess towards others. «اعطى... للیسرى، بخل... للعسرى»

11- Goodness brings goodness, and evil brings evil. «اعطى واتقى... فسنیسره للیسرى - بخل واستغنى... فسنیسره للعسرى»

12- Divine reward and punishment are commensurate with human behavior: if we solve the problems of the poor, Allah solves our problems. «فسنیسره للیسرى» And if we leave the poor in difficulty (usra), Allah also places difficulty in our path. «فسنیسره للعسرى»

13- The stingy should know that wealth is not a savior. «و مایغنى عنه ماله اذا تردّى»

14- The result of stinginess is downfall: downfall from humanity and perfection, downfall in the eyes of people, along with falling into Hellfire and being deprived of spiritual ranks in the Hereafter. «اذا تردّى»

15- Sometimes the source of today's stinginess is the fear of future poverty. «بَخل واستغنى» (Just as in verses 267 and 268 of Surah Al-Baqarah, alongside the command for spending, He says: It is Satan who frightens you with future poverty. «انفقوا... الشیطان یعدكم الفقر»)

16- Sometimes the source of stinginess is being arrogant about the world and feeling self-sufficient regarding the promises of Paradise. «بخل و استغنى»

79) Al-Ma'idah, 27. 80) An-Nisa, 95.

Ayahs

إِنَّ عَلَيْنَا لَلْهُدَىٰ (۱۲)
Indeed guidance rests with Us, (12)
وَإِنَّ لَنَا لَلْـَٔاخِرَةَ وَٱلْأُولَىٰ (۱۳)
and to Us belong the world and the Hereafter. (13)
فَأَنذَرْتُكُمْ نَارًا تَلَظَّىٰ (۱٤)
So I warn you of a blazing fire, (14)
لَا يَصْلَىٰهَآ إِلَّا ٱلْأَشْقَى (۱٥)
which none shall enter except the most wretched [of persons] (15)
ٱلَّذِى كَذَّبَ وَتَوَلَّىٰ (۱٦)
—he who impugns [God’s prophets] and turns his back. (16)
وَسَيُجَنَّبُهَا ٱلْأَتْقَى (۱۷)
The Godwary [person] will be spared of that (17)
ٱلَّذِى يُؤْتِى مَالَهُۥ يَتَزَكَّىٰ (۱۸)
—he who gives his wealth to purify himself (18)
وَمَا لِأَحَدٍ عِندَهُۥ مِن نِّعْمَةٍ تُجْزَىٰٓ (۱۹)
and does not expect any reward from anyone, (19)
إِلَّا ٱبْتِغَآءَ وَجْهِ رَبِّهِ ٱلْأَعْلَىٰ (۲٠)
but seek only the pleasure of their Lord, the Most Exalted, (20)
وَلَسَوْفَ يَرْضَىٰ (۲۱)
and, surely, soon they will be well-pleased. (21)

Key Points

Guidance from Allah is an absolute certainty, «انّ علینا للهدى», but its acceptance by the people is not certain. As He says elsewhere: «و امّا ثمود فهدیناهم فاستحبوا العمى على الهدى»(81) Taladhdhā (تلظّى) is a flame of fire without smoke, which has a greater burning intensity. Eternity and permanence in Hellfire are specific to the wretched individuals. «لایصلاها الاّ الاشقى» In the word ṣalā (صَلى), permanence and companionship are inherent. Perhaps «نارًا تلظّى», which is a specific type of fire, is reserved for the wretched individuals, while other criminals will be afflicted by other types of fire. The Ayah «و لسوف یرضى» can be interpreted in two ways: one, that man becomes satisfied with God upon reaching his goals; the other, that Allah becomes satisfied with him. Of course, in the Quran, both instances—Allah's satisfaction with man and man's satisfaction with Allah—are mentioned together: «رضى اللّه عنهم و رضوا عنه»(82), «ارجعى الى ربّك راضیة مرضیة»(83)

81) Fussilat, 17. 82) Al-Bayyinah, 8. 83) Al-Fajr, 28.

Messages

1- One of the Divine traditions that Allah has made incumbent upon Himself is the guidance of people through intellect, innate nature, and the Prophets. «انّ علینا للهدى»

2- Accepting guidance is for your own benefit; otherwise, Allah is self-sufficient. «و انّ لنا للآخرة و الاولى»

3- Warning and admonition are means of Divine guidance. «فانذرتكم» (Divine warnings must be taken seriously, as they come from the One in Whose Hand are the Hereafter and the present world.)

4- The Fire of the Hereafter is an unknown and tremendous matter. «ناراً» is presented in the indefinite form (nakirah).

5- Wretchedness, like piety (taqwa), has stages. «الاشقى ... الاتقى» (The one who ignores all those signs of guidance is the most wretched: «اشقى، الّذى كذّب و تولىَّ»)

6- Piety, accompanied by helping the deprived, is a shield against the Fire of Hell. «سیجنّبها الاتقى الّذى یؤتى ماله»

7- Helping the deprived is the constant practice of the pious. «یؤتى»

8- Giving aid from one's personal wealth is valuable. «ماله»

9- The sign of piety is sincere financial aid. «الاتقى الّذى یوتى ماله»

10- Helping the poor is a way for purification (tazkiyah) and self-building. «یؤتى ماله یتزكى»

11- Financial aid given to compensate for the favors of others is not a source of growth. «ما لاَحدٍ عنده من نعمة» (Charity (infāq) has high value when a person is not indebted to others and has no expectation in return.)

12- The pious person seeks nothing but the satisfaction of Allah. «الاّ ابتغاء وجه ربّه الاعلى»

13- The intention of seeking closeness (to Allah) is a necessary condition. «الاّ ابتغاء وجه ربّه»

14- Charity (infāq) must be in ways pleasing to Allah. «یؤتى ماله... ابتغاء وجه ربّه»

15- He is the Lord, the Most High (al-A‘lā); therefore, seek only His satisfaction, for He grants the highest reward. «ابتغاء وجه ربّه الاعلى»

16- The sincere person reaches the station of satisfaction (riḍā) and is always pleased with Allah. «و لسوف یرضى»
